story.iWant: Recipe to generate valid Capture One .costyle preset files from UniversalRecipe representation
story.soThat: I can convert my presets from other formats (XMP, lrtemplate, NP3) to Capture One styles and use them in Capture One Pro editing software
story.tasks: - Implement Generate() function with UniversalRecipe to CaptureOneStyle mapping - Implement scaling helper functions (scaleToInt, scaleToFloat, kelvinToC1Temperature) - Handle edge cases (nil recipe, empty recipe, out-of-range values, metadata preservation) - Format XML output with proper indentation and XML declaration - Write comprehensive unit tests (≥85% coverage) - Manual validation in Capture One Pro - Update documentation (parameter-mapping.md, testdata README)

story-context.acceptanceCriteria: - AC-1: Generate valid .costyle XML structure with required elements, namespaces, human-readable formatting - AC-2: Map UniversalRecipe fields to .costyle parameters with correct scaling (Exposure, Contrast, Saturation, Temperature, Tint, Clarity) - AC-3: Handle edge cases (missing parameters, out-of-range values clamped, metadata preserved, unsupported parameters skipped) - AC-4: Validate generated output (loads in Capture One Pro, parameter values match input, XML validates, round-trip 95%+ accuracy) - AC-5: Unit test coverage ≥85% for costyle/generate.go, all tests pass in CI

doc.title: Epic 8 Technical Specification: Capture One Format Support
doc.snippet: Defines CaptureOneStyle struct with XML tags, parameter ranges (Exposure -2.0/+2.0, Contrast/Saturation/etc -100/+100), and UniversalRecipe mapping strategy. Includes Generate() function signature and scaling formulas.

doc.title: Epic 8 Technical Specification
doc.snippet: Generate() creates .costyle XML from UniversalRecipe using xml.MarshalIndent, returns formatted XML bytes. Maps UniversalRecipe fields with range scaling (e.g., UR -1.0/+1.0 → C1 -100/+100 for contrast/saturation).

doc.title: Epic 8 Technical Specification
doc.snippet: Provides concrete implementations: scaleToInt() for contrast/saturation scaling, kelvinToC1Temperature() for temperature conversion (5500K reference, 60K per unit). Includes examples and clamping logic.

doc.title: Story 8-1: Costyle Parser
doc.snippet: Establishes package structure (internal/formats/costyle/), CaptureOneStyle struct definitions, parameter ranges, error handling pattern (fmt.Errorf with %w), and test coverage target (≥85%).

doc.title: Parameter Mapping Reference
doc.snippet: Documents parameter mappings and scaling formulas between UniversalRecipe and various formats. Will be updated with .costyle generation mappings showing reverse scaling (UR → Capture One).

artifact.reason: Proven pattern for XML generation from UniversalRecipe. Uses encoding/xml with MarshalIndent, prepends XML declaration, handles parameter mapping with validation. Same pattern applies to .costyle generation.
Defines ConversionError type (Operation, Format, Field, Cause) and XML struct pattern with namespace tags. Reuse error wrapping pattern for costyle/generate.go.
Example of binary format generator with parameter scaling, range validation, and edge case handling. Similar scaling logic needed for costyle integer parameters.
Defines UniversalRecipe struct with all fields (Exposure, Contrast, Saturation, Temperature, Tint, Clarity, etc.). Source for mapping to .costyle parameters.
Shows format detection and conversion routing pattern. Will be extended with costyle format case in generate switch statement (Story 8-5 integration).

go.package: Math.Round for float to int conversion in scaling functions

story-context.constraints: - MUST use encoding/xml stdlib package only (zero external dependencies) - MUST achieve ≥85% test coverage for generate.go - MUST follow ConversionError wrapping pattern from xmp/parse.go - MUST generate human-readable XML with 2-space indentation - MUST prepend XML declaration: <?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?> - MUST clamp out-of-range values to valid Capture One ranges - MUST omit zero-valued parameters for cleaner XML output - MUST reuse CaptureOneStyle struct from Story 8-1 (types.go) - MUST include scaling helper functions in generate.go - Target performance: <100ms per conversion - Code must pass gofmt and go vet without warnings

interface.name: scaleToInt
interface.signature: func scaleToInt(value float64, min, max int) int

interface.name: scaleToFloat
interface.signature: func scaleToFloat(value float64, min, max float64) float64

interface.name: CaptureOneStyle (reuse from 8-1)
interface.signature: type CaptureOneStyle struct { XMLName xml.Name; RDF RDF }

tests.standards: Follow Recipe project testing standards: table-driven tests, ≥85% coverage target, use testdata/ for sample files, benchmark tests for performance validation (<100ms target), ConversionError validation in error cases. All tests must pass in CI pipeline.
tests.locations: - internal/formats/costyle/generate_test.go (new file) - testdata/costyle/ (sample .costyle files for round-trip testing, from Story 8-1)
tests.ideas: - TestGenerate_ValidRecipe: Generate from populated UniversalRecipe, verify XML well-formed and parameter values match input - TestGenerate_EmptyRecipe: Generate from empty UniversalRecipe (all zero values), verify minimal valid .costyle with neutral parameters - TestGenerate_OutOfRangeValues: Test clamping behavior (Exposure=5.0 should clamp to 2.0) - TestGenerate_MetadataPreservation: Verify name/author/description fields preserved from Metadata map - TestGenerate_RoundTrip: Parse sample .costyle → Generate → Parse again, compare 95%+ match - TestScalingFunctions: Unit tests for scaleToInt/scaleToFloat/kelvinToC1Temperature helpers - BenchmarkGenerate: Verify performance <100ms (likely much faster, similar to XMP)
